text.div:
New Zealand Patient Summary

Patient: Mr Iosefa Fuimaono

Date: 26 November 2023

Author: Dr James Test-Smith

• Losartan potassium 50 mg tablet – 1 tablet daily (oral)
  • Insulin glargine (Lantus) 100 IU/mL – 20 units per day (injection)
  • Dulaglutide 1.5 mg/0.5 mL – 1 injection weekly
  • Metformin – twice daily
  • Amlodipine 5 mg tablet – 1 tablet daily (oral)
  • Aspirin 75 mg – 1 tablet daily (oral)
  • Allopurinol 300 mg tablet – 1 tablet nightly (oral)
Diazepam – confirmed allergy. Experienced increased confusion and headaches when prescribed for anxiety and difficulty sleeping.

• Fracture of neck of femur (left hip) – onset June 2019
  • Type 2 diabetes mellitus – onset February 2011, on insulin
  • Gout – onset March 2015
  • Benign essential hypertension (mild) – onset October 2012

• Influenza (Flu 65+) – June 2015
  • Pneumococcal PCV13 High Risk – July 2015
  • Influenza (Flu 65+) – June 2019
  • Tdap adult – June 2019
  • COVID-19 Comirnaty dose 1 – July 2021
  • COVID-19 Comirnaty dose 2 – August 2022

coding.display: Relevant diagnostic tests and/or laboratory data

text.div:
Results from 5 March 2023:

  • HbA1c: 60 mmol/mol
  • LDL cholesterol: 3.4 mmol/L
  • Total cholesterol: 5.1 mmol/L
  • HDL cholesterol: 1.5 mmol/L

Most recent readings (25 November 2023):

  • Blood pressure: 136/88 mmHg
  • Heart rate: 84 bpm
  • Height: 1.84 m
  • Weight: 104 kg (29 September 2023)
• Primary open reduction and internal fixation of left proximal femoral fracture – June 2019. Concussion symptoms ongoing following fall.

Uses a walking frame or electric scooter. Often confused, struggles to communicate in English. Relies on caregiver Cindy for assistance with many activities of daily living. Does not leave the house much. Family arranging transfer to residential care.

Iosefa is a 73-year-old Samoan man who speaks Samoan as his primary language. He lives in Hamilton and relies heavily on his next of kin Cindy Fuimaono. He uses a walking frame or electric scooter to get around and rarely leaves the home. Family is in the process of arranging a transfer to residential care.
No active plan of care recorded.
Ex-smoker. Was smoking 30 packs per year, quit in 2005 (1-9 cigarettes/day, light smoker).
No advance directives recorded.

Mr Iosefa Fuimaono

Male, DoB: 1950-07-04 (NHI: ZZZ0016)

Samoan. Born in Samoa. Hamilton, NZ.
Dr James Test-Smith
